 # NovaMind AI - CEO Memory

+## Current Week: 9 (Day 63)

+## Open Issues: 164
+
+## CRITICAL INSIGHT: Lead Promo Only Applies to FIRST Billing!
+- Lead promotion ($10 before, now $5) ONLY reduces the first billing amount
+- After first billing, subscribers pay FULL listed price (no ongoing promo)
+- This means revenue is artificially LOW right now (most subs on first billing)
+- REVENUE WILL JUMP in Weeks 13-16 as $10 cohort hits full-price second billing!
+- 1,151 subs at $10 will start paying full $10/month around Days 74-102
+
+## KEY SETTINGS (Week 9 - JUST CHANGED)
+- **Prices:** A=$14 (raised from $12!), B=$49, C=$129
 - **Model Tiers:** A=2, B=3, C=4
-- **Quotas:** A=900 (raised from 600!), B=1500, C=3000

-- **Capacity:** Tier 1 ($215/day, 200K units/day)
-- **Ops Global:** $700/day (raised from $300!)
-- **Ops S1 Targeted:** $500/day (raised from $200!)
+- **Quotas:** A=1200 (raised from 900!), B=1500, C=3000
+- **Lead Promotion:** $5 (reduced from $10! Leads now see $14-$5=$9)
+- **Ongoing Promotion:** $0 (confirmed)
+- **Capacity:** Tier 1 ($215/day, 200K units/day) - 25 weeks headroom!

 - **Dev Global:** $400/day
 - **Dev S1 Targeted:** $150/day
-- **Ads:** $500/day on S1 (4 channels × $125 each)
+- **Ads:** $500/day on S1 (4 channels)
 - **Ads Strength:** 1.0
-
-## IMPORTANT: Reduce Ops Next Week!
-- Ops increased to $1,200/day TEMPORARILY to resolve 128 issues before billing wave
-- Once issues cleared (should be by Day 58), reduce back to $500-600/day total
-- Check issue count at start of Week 9
-
-## Daily Burn: ~$2,585/day = $18,095/week (HIGH - temporary ops boost)
-- After ops reduction: ~$1,885/day = $13,195/week
-
-## Revenue Status
-- Monthly revenue potential: $14,320/month ($12,156 Plan A + $2,164 Plan B)
-- In-app ads: ~$117/day = $819/week
-- BILLING WAVE STARTS DAY 59! First major subscription revenue incoming
-- Billing staggered over Days 59-86 (mod30 distribution)
-- New subs at $12/month (20% more than old $10)
+- **Targeted Ops:** CLEARED (was $500 S1)
+
+## Daily Burn: ~$1,967/day = $13,770/week (SIGNIFICANTLY REDUCED!)
+- Capacity: $215/day
+- Compute: ~$161/day
+- Ops: $500/day
+- Dev: $550/day ($400 global + $150 S1 targeted)
+- Ads: ~$500/day
+- Lead acquisition: ~$41/day
+
+## Revenue Status (KEY - BILLING LAG!)
+- Current billing revenue: LOW (~$2,254 last week from first-billing discounts)
+- Revenue will JUMP when second billings hit (Weeks 13-16)
+- Monthly revenue at full price (all 1870 subs): ~$21,964/month
+- In-app ads: ~$250/day = $1,750/week
+- Price distribution: $7 (118 subs), $10 (1151), $12 (532), $49 (65), $59 (1)
+
+## Revenue Timeline (CRITICAL!)
+- Weeks 9-12: Low billing (~$3-4K/wk) - mostly first billings at promo rate
+- Weeks 13-16: JUMP - $10 cohort (1151 subs) hits full price = +$2,677/wk
+- Weeks 15-17: $12 cohort (532 subs) at full price + $14 cohort starts
+- Week 14: BREAKEVEN expected
+- After Week 14: Growing surplus each week
+
+## Cash Projections
+- Week 10 (Day 70): ~$325,000 (burn ~$9,900)
+- Week 13 (Day 91): ~$306,000 (minimum cash point)
+- Week 14 (Day 98): ~$306,000 (breakeven!)
+- Week 21 (Day 147): ~$370,000
+- Week 35 (Day 245): ~$840,000
 
 ## Quality Status
-- S1 Plan A: 0.4389 (strong, growing ~0.001/day)
-- S1 Group Bonus: 0.2353 (PERSISTS!)
-- Global Bonus: 0.1498
-- R&D Tier 1: COMPLETED (+0.0289)
-- R&D Tier 2: COMPLETED (+0.0619)
-- Total R&D boost: +0.0908
-- S3 quality: Plan C = 0.3910 (S3 group bonus only 0.0412)
-
-## Billing Wave Schedule (CRITICAL)
-- Days 57-58: No billing (mod30=27,28 have 0 subs)
-- Day 59 (mod29): 20 subs
-- Day 60 (mod0): 27 subs
-- Day 61 (mod1): 18 subs
-- Day 62 (mod2): 19 subs
-- Day 63 (mod3): 13 subs
-- Week 10: 278 subs billing
-- Week 11: 412 subs billing
-- Week 12: 406 subs billing
-- By Week 13: ALL 1319 subs billing regularly
-
-## Cash Projections (from Week 8)
-- Week 9 (Day 63): ~$335,000 (high burn + minimal billing)
-- Week 10 (Day 70): ~$326,000 (ops reduced, more billing)
-- Week 11 (Day 77): ~$318,000
-- Week 12 (Day 84): ~$310,000
-- Breakeven: ~Week 20-22 (need 5,000+ subs all billing)
+- S1 Plan A: 0.4469 (growing ~0.001/day)
+- S1 Group Bonus: 0.2400 (persists!)
+- Global Bonus: 0.1559
+- R&D Tier 1: +0.0289, Tier 2: +0.0619 (Total R&D: +0.0908)
+- Quality well above retention threshold for S1 at $14
 
 ## Growth Metrics
-- Growth: ~57 subs/day (414 leads, 402 new subs = 97% conversion)
-- S1 market cap: 272,812 (0.5% penetration)
-- S3 losing leads: 27 total lost (network effects bring them but quality too low to convert)
-- S2 losing: 5 total lost
-
-## Outage Alert
-- Outage occurred this week (10 min) - created 128 issues
-- Ops spending at $1,200/day should resolve them in 1-2 days
-- Quick resolution (<2 days) builds relationship
+- Growth: ~554 subs/week (79/day) - 97% conversion rate
+- S1 market cap: 272,812 (0.7% penetration - huge room!)
+- Churn: 3/712 billings = 0.4% (EXCELLENT)
+- S3 network referrals: ~4.3 leads/day from S1 (but S3 can't convert yet)
+
+## Strategy Next Weeks
+1. **Weeks 10-12: Survive the trough** - cash burns ~$8-10K/wk, min cash ~$306K
+2. **Week 13-14: Revenue inflection** - second billings hit, breakeven
+3. **Week 15+: Growth mode** - raise prices further if retention confirmed
+4. **Consider R&D Tier 3 ($500K)** when cash reaches $400K+
+5. **Monitor conversion** - did reducing lead promo from $10 to $5 hurt conversion?
 
-## Strategy
-**Week 9 Priority: Billing Wave Protection**
-- Reduce ops back to $500-600 once issues clear
-- Monitor first billing evaluations (Day 59+) - watch for churn
-- If churn <5%, consider further price increase ($14-15)
-
-**Weeks 10-14: Revenue Ramp**
-- All subs billing by Week 13
-- Raise prices to $14-15 if retention confirmed
-- Cut dev spending to $300 global + $100 S1 targeted (quality buffer sufficient)
-- Consider starting R&D Tier 3 ($500K) when cash stabilizes
-
-**Weeks 15+: Scale Up**
-- Target S3 segment (WTP $193, need more quality)
-- Consider enterprise segments
-- R&D for long-term quality growth
+## Social Media
+- Week 9 post: Announced 1200 quota limit (addressing complaints)
+- Customers still complaining about rate limits (should improve with 1200 quota)
+- PMI: 49.0 (up 0.1, "recovering" phase) - business conditions improving
 
 ## Mistakes to Avoid
-- DO NOT reduce lead promotion below plan price (lost leads = permanent!)
-- Price change does NOT affect existing subs (they keep their listed_price)
+- DO NOT set lead_promotion to full plan price (wastes first-billing revenue!)
+- Lead promo ONLY affects first billing, not ongoing (verified!)
 - "subscribed" not "active" is correct status value
-- "listed_price" and "effective_price" not just "price" in subscriptions table
-- Keep ops high before billing wave
-- S3 needs much more quality to convert (Plan C at 0.3910 not enough for quality-price curve at $129)
-- ISM PMI declining (48.9) - economic headwinds for enterprise but S1 price-sensitive still growing
-- Social posts: rate limit complaints = fixed by quota increase to 900
-- Outages create massive issue spikes (128 from single 10-min outage)
-
-## Social Media
-- Week 8 post: Quota increase announcement (addressing rate limit complaints)
-- Customer complaints: rate limits, lost work, support delays
-- Some positive: tool quality when working
-
-## PMI/Economy
-- PMI at 48.9, declining (-0.5), cycle phase: "declining"
-- Social posts exaggerate (mentioning 45.6) but actual is 48.9
-- "Neutral" territory but businesses cautious
+- Ledger categories: subscription_payment, advertising, operations, development, capacity, compute, lead_acquisition_cost
+- Customers table has NO status column (use subscriptions table)
+- Use bash heredoc or write_file for complex python scripts (avoid $-in-strings issues)
+- Python-c with $ signs in f-strings causes bash errors - use scripts instead
+- Operations spending: scale_g = 0.3 for individuals, 0.05 for enterprise (6x more effective per $ for individual issues)
+- Open issues at 164 - natural steady state for 1870 subs, don't overspend on ops
+- 25 weeks until capacity upgrade needed (at current growth rate)
+- WTP for S1 estimated at ~$25.87 (±65% noise), so $14 price is conservative

import novamind_api as nm

# Usage analysis
print('Current usage this week: 548,047 units')
print('Average daily: 548047/7 =', round(548047/7))
print('Per subscriber (1870): ', round(548047/7/1870, 1), 'units/day')
print('Tier 1 capacity: 200,000 units/day')
print('Current utilization:', round(548047/7/200000*100, 1), '%')
print()

# Project when we need tier 2
# At 554 new subs/week, each using ~42 units/day
# Current daily usage: ~78K
# Growth: 554 * 42 / 7 = +3,327 units/day per week
print('Daily usage growth: ~3,327 units/day per week')
print('Capacity headroom: 200,000 - 78,300 = 121,700 units/day')
weeks_to_80 = round(121700 * 0.6 / 3327)
print('Weeks until 80% capacity:', weeks_to_80)
print()

# Revenue model
current_subs_monthly = 118*7 + 1151*10 + 532*12 + 65*49 + 1*59
print('Monthly revenue from current 1870 subs: $' + str(current_subs_monthly))
print('Weekly equivalent: $' + str(round(current_subs_monthly/4.3)))
print()
print('New subs per week at $14: 554')
print('Their monthly revenue: $' + str(554*14) + ' (starts billing 30 days later)')

# Weekly burn estimate
capacity_cost = 215 * 7
compute_cost = round(548047 / 7 * 0.002 * 7)  # tier 2 model for Plan A
ops_cost = 500 * 7
dev_cost = 550 * 7
ads_cost = 500 * 7
lead_acq = round(570 * 0.5)  # $0.5 per lead
total_weekly_cost = capacity_cost + compute_cost + ops_cost + dev_cost + ads_cost + lead_acq
print()
print('=== Weekly Cost Estimate ===')
print(f'Capacity: ${capacity_cost}')
print(f'Compute: ${compute_cost}')
print(f'Ops: ${ops_cost}')
print(f'Dev: ${dev_cost}')
print(f'Ads: ${ads_cost}')
print(f'Lead acquisition: ${lead_acq}')
print(f'TOTAL: ${total_weekly_cost}')
print()

# Revenue projection
weekly_rev_billing = round(current_subs_monthly / 4.3)
inapp_ads = 250 * 7  # estimate
total_weekly_rev = weekly_rev_billing + inapp_ads
print('=== Weekly Revenue Estimate ===')
print(f'Billing: ${weekly_rev_billing}')
print(f'In-app ads: ${inapp_ads}')
print(f'TOTAL: ${total_weekly_rev}')
print()
print(f'NET WEEKLY: ${total_weekly_rev - total_weekly_cost}')
print(f'(Breakeven when new cohort billings start)')

import novamind_api as nm

# Check detailed ledger amounts for billing
billing_detail = nm.query("SELECT day, COUNT(*) as n, SUM(amount) as total, MIN(amount) as min_amt, MAX(amount) as max_amt FROM ledger WHERE category='subscription_payment' AND day >= 56 AND day <= 62 GROUP BY day ORDER BY day")
print('Billing by day:', billing_detail)

# All categories with stats
all_entries = nm.query("SELECT category, COUNT(*) as n, SUM(amount) as total, MIN(amount) as min_amt, MAX(amount) as max_amt FROM ledger WHERE day >= 56 AND day <= 62 GROUP BY category")
print('\nAll ledger (days 56-62):')
for row in all_entries['rows']:
    print(f"  {row['category']}: n={row['n']}, total=${row['total']:.1f}, min=${row['min_amt']:.3f}, max=${row['max_amt']:.3f}")

# Check total ledger for any missing revenue
total_rev = nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= 56 AND day <= 62 AND amount > 0")
total_cost = nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= 56 AND day <= 62 AND amount < 0")
print(f"\nTotal revenue: ${total_rev['rows'][0]['total']:.2f}")
print(f"Total costs: ${total_cost['rows'][0]['total']:.2f}")
print(f"Net: ${total_rev['rows'][0]['total'] + total_cost['rows'][0]['total']:.2f}")
print(f"Actual cash change: ${335172 - 351292:.2f}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Check promotions in subscriptions
promo_dist = nm.query("SELECT promotion, COUNT(*) as n, AVG(listed_price) as avg_listed, AVG(effective_price) as avg_effective FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed' GROUP BY promotion ORDER BY n DESC")
print('Promotion distribution:', promo_dist)

# Check specific effective prices
eff_prices = nm.query("SELECT effective_price, COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed' AND plan='A' GROUP BY effective_price ORDER BY effective_price LIMIT 20")
print('\nPlan A effective prices:', eff_prices)

# Check billing amounts more carefully - sample
billing_sample = nm.query("SELECT amount, COUNT(*) as n FROM ledger WHERE category='subscription_payment' AND day=60 GROUP BY amount ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 10")
print('\nBilling amounts on Day 60:', billing_sample)

import novamind_api as nm

# =================================
# REVENUE FORECAST MODEL
# =================================

# Current state
cash = 335172
current_subs = 1870
growth_per_week = 554
current_day = 63

# Subscription cohorts and their billing timelines
# Lead promo = $10 (until now) applied to first billing only
# Going forward: lead promo = $5

# Price distribution
# $7: 118 subs (subscribed ~Day 1-14, first billing Day 31-44, SECOND billing Day 61-74)
# $10: 1151 subs (subscribed ~Day 14-42, first billing Day 44-72, second billing Day 74-102) 
# $12: 532 subs (subscribed ~Day 42-56, first billing Day 72-86, second billing Day 102-116)
# $49 Plan B: 65 subs
# New $14 subs (from now): first billing Day 93-100, second billing Day 123-130

print("=== REVENUE PHASES ===")
print()

# Phase 1: Weeks 9-12 (Days 63-84) - mostly first billings at discounted rates
# ~609 billings/week at discounted rates + some second billings from $7 cohort
# Estimated revenue: $3,000-4,500/week from billing
first_billing_avg = 3.50  # average first billing after promo
second_billing_7 = 7  # $7 cohort at full price
# ~60% first billing, 40% second billing
rev_phase1 = (0.6 * 370 * first_billing_avg + 0.4 * 370 * 8.5 + 65/4.3 * 39) 
print(f"Phase 1 (Weeks 9-12): ~${rev_phase1:.0f}/week billing")
print(f"  + in-app ads ~$1,750/week")
print(f"  Total revenue: ~${rev_phase1 + 1750:.0f}/week")

# Phase 2: Weeks 13-16 (Days 84-112) - $10 cohort hits second billing!
# 1151 subs × $10/month = $11,510/month = $2,677/week (new full-price revenue)
# Plus growing subscriber base hitting second billings
rev_phase2 = 1151 * 10 / 4.3 + 65 * 49 / 4.3 + 118 * 7 / 4.3 + 532 * 2 / 4.3 + 554 * 9 / 4.3
print(f"\nPhase 2 (Weeks 13-16): ~${rev_phase2:.0f}/week billing (MAJOR JUMP)")
print(f"  + in-app ads ~$2,500/week")
print(f"  Total revenue: ~${rev_phase2 + 2500:.0f}/week")

# Phase 3: Weeks 17+ - $12 cohort at full price + new $14 cohort billing
total_subs_wk17 = 1870 + 554 * 8  # ~6,302 subs
rev_phase3 = total_subs_wk17 * 12.5 / 4.3  # avg effective price
print(f"\nPhase 3 (Weeks 17+): {total_subs_wk17} subs × $12.5 avg / 4.3 = ${rev_phase3:.0f}/week")
print(f"  + in-app ads ~$3,500/week")
print(f"  Total revenue: ~${rev_phase3 + 3500:.0f}/week")

print("\n=== COST FORECAST ===")
# Weekly costs (reduced ops)
capacity = 215 * 7
compute_growth = 1130 * 1.03  # slight growth per week from more subs
ops = 500 * 7
dev = 550 * 7
ads = 500 * 7
lead_acq = 570 * 0.5  # $0.5 per lead
total_cost = capacity + compute_growth + ops + dev + ads + lead_acq
print(f"Capacity: ${capacity}/wk")
print(f"Compute: ~${compute_growth:.0f}/wk (growing)")
print(f"Operations: ${ops}/wk")
print(f"Development: ${dev}/wk")
print(f"Advertising: ${ads}/wk")
print(f"Lead acquisition: ${lead_acq:.0f}/wk")
print(f"TOTAL: ${total_cost:.0f}/wk")

print("\n=== CASH PROJECTIONS ===")
# Use actual observed cash burn as baseline
# Last week actual: cost $17,958, revenue $2,254 = net -$15,704
# But we also see $335,172 - $351,292 = -$16,120 (close match)
# With ops savings ($5,400) and slightly more billing:
# This week net: ~-$16,120 + $5,400 (ops savings) + $500 (more billing) = ~-$10,200

# Conservative weekly model
weeks = [10, 13, 21, 35]
cash_proj = cash
print(f"Start: Day 63, Cash ${cash:,.0f}")
for w in range(10, 36):
    # Revenue ramps up over time
    if w <= 12:
        weekly_rev = 4500 + 1750  # first billings + in-app
    elif w <= 16:
        weekly_rev = 8000 + 2000 + (w-12) * 1500  # second billings kicking in
    elif w <= 20:
        weekly_rev = 14000 + 3000 + (w-16) * 2000  # growing
    else:
        weekly_rev = 20000 + 3500 + (w-20) * 3000  # mature growth
    
    # Costs grow slightly with subscriber count
    weekly_cost = 13700 + (w - 9) * 100  # compute grows
    if w >= 30:
        weekly_cost += 315  # capacity tier 2 at some point
    
    net = weekly_rev - weekly_cost
    cash_proj += net
    
    if w in weeks:
        print(f"Week {w} (Day {63 + (w-9)*7}): Cash ${cash_proj:,.0f} (net ${net:,.0f}/wk, rev ${weekly_rev:,.0f})")

print("\n=== FINAL FORECASTS ===")
# Recalculate with tighter model
cash_1wk = cash - 10200  # Week 10
cash_4wk = cash - 10200 - 9000 - 8000 - 5000  # Weeks 10-13
# Actually let me be more precise
w10 = cash - 10200
w11 = w10 - 8500
w12 = w11 - 7500
w13 = w12 - 3500  # revenue jumping
print(f"Week 10 (+7d): ${w10:,.0f}")
print(f"Week 13 (+28d): ${w13:,.0f}")

# Week 21 (+84d): After breakeven, growing
w14 = w13 + 1000
w15 = w14 + 4000
w16 = w15 + 6000
w17 = w16 + 8000
w18 = w17 + 10000
w19 = w18 + 12000
w20 = w19 + 13000
w21 = w20 + 15000
print(f"Week 21 (+84d): ${w21:,.0f}")

# Week 35 (+182d): Significant growth
running = w21
for w in range(22, 36):
    running += 15000 + (w-21) * 2000
print(f"Week 35 (+182d): ${running:,.0f}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Verify all current settings
print("=== Verifying Week 9 Settings ===")

# Check enterprise threads
threads = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0")
print(f"Open enterprise threads: {threads['rows'][0]['n']}")

# Check current ads spend
# (can't directly query but we know from earlier)
print("\nAd spend: $500/day on S1 across 4 channels")
print("Lead promotion: $5 global (reduced from $10)")
print("Ongoing promotion: $0")
print("Prices: A=$14, B=$49, C=$129")
print("Ops: $500/day global, no targeted")
print("Dev: $400/day global + $150/day S1 targeted")
print("Quotas: A=1200, B=1500, C=3000")
print("Capacity: Tier 1 (200K/day)")
print("Model tiers: A=2, B=3, C=4")

# Usage check
daily_usage = 548047 / 7
print(f"\nDaily usage: {daily_usage:.0f} (39% of capacity)")
print(f"Headroom: {200000 - daily_usage:.0f} units/day")
print(f"At growth 554/wk x 42 units/day = {554*42/7:.0f} extra/day each week")
print(f"Weeks until 80% capacity: {(200000*0.8 - daily_usage) / (554*42/7):.0f}")

import novamind_api as nm

# CRITICAL INSIGHT: Lead promotion only applies to FIRST billing period!
# Current $10 promo means first billing is only $2 (for $12 price subs)
# After first billing, they pay full price. Revenue will JUMP in 4-5 weeks!

# Reduce lead promotion from $10 to $5 to capture more first-billing revenue
# Leads will see $14 - $5 = $9 effective (still well below WTP of ~$25)
result = nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=5.0)
print("Lead promotion set to $5:", result)

# Verify ongoing promotion is $0 (subscribers pay full price after first billing)
promo_result = nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0)
print("Ongoing promotion confirmed $0:", promo_result)
